code:
import sys

def main():
    import sys
    input = sys.stdin.read
    data = input().split()
    
    N = int(data[0])
    S = data[1]
    C = list(map(int, data[2:]))
    
    # We need exactly one pair of adjacent equal characters
    # So, the string must have exactly one place where T[i] == T[i+1]
    
    # We will consider all possible positions for the equal pair
    # For each possible position i (from 0 to N-2), we will:
    # - Make T[i] == T[i+1]
    # - Make sure all other adjacent pairs are different
    
    min_cost = float('inf')
    
    for i in range(N - 1):
        # We will make T[i] == T[i+1]
        # So we need to make sure that all other adjacent pairs are different
        
        # We will try both possibilities for T[i] and T[i+1]
        for val in [0, 1]:
            cost = 0
            # Set T[i] and T[i+1] to val
            if S[i] != str(val):
                cost += C[i]
            if S[i+1] != str(val):
                cost += C[i+1]
            
            # Now, for all other positions j != i, j != i+1, we need T[j] != T[j+1]
            # So we will set T[j] and T[j+1] to be different
            for j in range(N - 1):
                if j == i or j == i + 1:
                    continue
                # We need T[j] != T[j+1]
                # So we can choose either T[j] = 0 and T[j+1] = 1 or vice versa
                # We will choose the one with minimal cost
                cost1 = 0
                if S[j] != '0':
                    cost1 += C[j]
                if S[j+1] != '1':
                    cost1 += C[j+1]
                cost2 = 0
                if S[j] != '1':
                    cost2 += C[j]
                if S[j+1] != '0':
                    cost2 += C[j+1]
                cost += min(cost1, cost2)
            
            min_cost = min(min_cost, cost)
    
    print(min_cost)

main()
